vector_metrics.basis: S_t reprojected from MODEL_WEIGHTS (fatigue+tactical+psychology); L_t mu_bias from DC-lambda residuals, sigma_chaos from weather+referee

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.note: The most likely flow — CAI's consensus across form, tactics and momentum.

scenarios.label: Upside · England click
scenarios.note: England's current form holds and they convert their chances — the tie opens up and they pull clear.

scenarios.label: Downside · Norway frustrates
scenarios.note: A flat, low-margin night — Norway sit in and the tie tightens, live to swing on extra time or penalties.

Norway: Goalkeeping is a soft spot under sustained pressure.
Blunter in the final third — fewer clear chances created.
England: No glaring weakness — a balanced side on current form.

match_flow.text: Attacking substitutions as both benches go for it
Level at full time 1-1 — into extra time
Fatigue tells — England find a winner in extra time

turning_points: Level at full time 1-1 — into extra time
Fatigue tells — England find a winner in extra time
risk_factors: Margins are razor-thin — a single moment likely settles it.
Model confidence is modest (39/100) — wide outcome spread.
Norway's goalkeeping is a relative weak point under pressure.

reasons: England carries the sharper attack (3.50 vs 2.54 xG/match).
England grades higher on form & big-match composure (40% vs 35%).
Edge from the spot: England convert ~78% vs Norway ~77% — decisive if it goes to pens.
Current form favours England — group record 4W-1D-0L (+21 Elo) vs Norway 4W-0D-1L (+33).
explainability.why_penalties: A shootout is unlikely (13%) — one side should settle it inside 120'.
explainability.what_could_change: An early goal swings momentum; an injury to England's key creator, or Norway stealing a set-piece, flips it.

disclaimer: This platform is an AI-driven probability simulation built for entertainment and analytics purposes. Not betting advice.
